repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
titles_tesim: Liturgy of Basil the Great and of the Presanctified Gifts
contents_tesim: Liturgy of Basil the Great and of the Presanctified Gifts

The preserved sections from the Liturgy of Saint Basil come from the anaphora and the dismissal rite. A notable gap occurs between fol. 142v and fol. 145r: among other items, the epiclesis and intercessions for the living and the dead (diptychs) are missing. The beginning of the Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts is found on fol. 146v, line 19
full_text_tesim: Each side preserves 22–23 lines of text arranged in a single column and spaced 6 mm apart
Liturgy of Basil the Great and of the Presanctified Gifts

Minuscule "à μεν distendu" | Variant of the epsilon style characterized by thick vertical strokes, triangular α, θ in the shape of biblical majuscule, and featuring the ligature "distendu" of μεν and γεν. Initials and titles are written in epigraphische Auszeichnungsmajuskel

One folio of Sin. gr. 960 corresponds to a single folio of the original manuscript, the likely size of the original being 180x140 mm (though doubtless with some trimming of the height). The Schriftspiegel is approximately 125x95 mm. The outer margin measures 30 mm, the lower 24 mm, and the upper 14 mm
The lower writing runs parallel to the overtext
